questions.question: What does LSB (Least Significant Bit) encoding primarily rely on to hide data in images?
questions.explanation: LSB encoding hides data by modifying the least significant bits of the least significant bits of the image pixels

questions.question: Which method of steganography involves rearranging the bits of the image pixels to hide data?
questions.explanation: BPCS Steganography rearranges the bits of the image pixels to hide data

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using Lossless data hiding in images?
questions.explanation: Lossless data hiding does not distort the image and retains its original quality

questions.question: Which of the following is a limitation of LSB encoding?
questions.explanation: LSB encoding is sensitive to noise and cannot be used to hide large amounts of data

questions.question: What is the primary application of BPCS Steganography?
questions.explanation: BPCS Steganography is primarily used for data hiding in images
